title: Additive Identity (Zero)
description: Recognize that adding zero to a number results in the same number.
condition: Addition expression of the form a + 0 or 0 + a.
response: State that the sum equals a.
label: Principle
notes: Based on identity property of addition.

title: Count All from Visual Representation
description: Count all objects in a pictured set to determine the total.
condition: A visual representation (e.g., dots or circles) showing two groups combined.
response: Count each object and state the total number.
label: Production / Skill
example: Counting 5 dots and 6 dots shown together to get $11$.
notes: Used when coloring and counting circles.

title: Doubles Facts
description: Recognize and compute sums where both addends are the same.
condition: Addition expression of the form a + a.
label: Principle
notes: Used directly and to support near-doubles reasoning.

title: Make-Ten Strategy
description: Decompose one addend to make a ten with the other addend, then add the remainder.
condition: Addition expression a + b where one addend can be decomposed to complete 10 with the other (e.g., 8 + 6, 7 + 6, 9 + 1).
response: Rewrite as (a + part) + remainder to make 10, then compute the total.
label: Principle
notes: Explicitly shown in solutions such as $6 + 6 + 2 = 14$ and $4 + 4 + 2 = 10$.

title: Near Doubles Strategy
description: Use a known doubles fact to solve an addition problem where the addends differ by 1 or 2.
condition: Addition expression a + b where a and b are close in value (e.g., 5 + 6, 6 + 7).
response: Rewrite using a doubles fact plus or minus the difference, then compute the total.
label: Principle
notes: Supported visually by circling doubles in the diagrams.
